history.hypothesis:
def predict(key, box) -> bool:
    # Hypothesis from teacher's rule: a box opens with the key of the same color.
    # Ignore numbers/shapes; use color match only.
    return key.color == box.color

history.hypothesis:
def predict(key, box) -> bool:
    # Hypothesis: boxes open with keys that match their printed shapes,
    # except for the red box (moon) which has no matching shape key and thus uses the red color key.
    if box.id == "red":
        return key.id == "red"
    if box.id == "pink":      # cloud shape
        return key.id == "cloud"
    if box.id == "white":     # diamond shape
        return key.id == "diamond"
    if box.id == "purple":    # heart shape
        return key.id == "heart"
    if box.id == "blue":      # triangle shape
        return key.id == "triangle"
    return False
history.llm_response:
def predict(key, box) -> bool:
    # Hypothesis: boxes open with keys that match their printed shapes,
    # except for the red box (moon) which has no matching shape key and thus uses the red color key.
    if box.id == "red":
        return key.id == "red"
    if box.id == "pink":      # cloud shape
        return key.id == "cloud"
    if box.id == "white":     # diamond shape
        return key.id == "diamond"
    if box.id == "purple":    # heart shape
        return key.id == "heart"
    if box.id == "blue":      # triangle shape
        return key.id == "triangle"
    return False
